返回本批次

一些不寻常的树

Some Unusual Trees

thoughts.wyounas.com 作者 simplegeek 分数 269 评论 78 2026/04/04 17:04
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 我最喜欢的消遣之一是探索旧书店。几个月前，我发现了一套完整的《大英百科全书》，第 15 版，出版于 1975 年，但那一套对我来说太贵了。然而，几周前，我在另一家书店发现了很优惠的价格并买了它。到目前为止这是一次多么美妙的经历啊。那些厚厚的书里有太多我不知道的东西。最近，我在读宇宙学时，同一卷中有关树木的条目引起了我的兴趣。我很惊喜地了解到一些不寻常的树木，并认为我应该分享它们，尽管它与我通常写的有点不同。让我们从沿海地区和河岸的红树林开始。红树林很不寻常，而且有点违反直觉：它们向海扩展，同时也帮助保卫背后的土地。这些树木是抵御海岸破坏的重要防御手段。红树林，照片：Ariefrahman，（CC BY-SA 4.0）它们的种子长成幼苗，当这些幼苗掉落时，它们要么固定在泥土中，要么漂到另一个地方。榕树虽然是一棵树，但看起来却像一整片森林。树根从树枝上落下，扎根在地下，长成像树干一样的新支撑物。榕树，照片：Kiran Gopi，（CC BY-SA 4.0）我很惊讶地发现，这样一棵树周长为 2,000 英尺，据估计可以容纳 20,000 人。这是大量的阴影。印度有几棵巨大的榕树。安得拉邦的 Thimmamma Marrimanu 目前保持着吉尼斯树冠纪录，面积约为 5.41 英亩（相当于三个足球场的面积）；加尔各答的大榕树是另一个著名的标本，占地约 4 英亩。一棵覆盖这么大面积的树完全违背了我对一棵树的期望。榕树占地 4.7 英亩，照片：PJeganathan，（CC BY-SA 4.0）ombú 大小像树，但形状如此肿胀和巨大，感觉几乎是建筑。 ombú 树高 60 英尺，宽 100 英尺，树干宽阔。 Ombú，照片：Roberto Fiadone，（CC BY-SA 4.0）它的树干和根部足够粗，在某些情况下足以让人坐在上面。旅行者的树看起来像棕榈树，但从植物学角度来看，它不是真正的棕榈树。马达加斯加的旅人树呈扇形，树干高达 30 英尺。叶扇由 30 至 45 片叶子组成，每片长达 36 英尺。他们的扇子看起来很雄伟。马达加斯加的旅行者之树，照片：Dicklyon，（CC，BY-SA 4.0）它的叶子有中空的基部，据报道，旅行者可以从中获取便携式水。它可以在叶基部保持水分，以抵御干燥的条件。塔里波棕榈扭转了树木生命的通常情况：它花费数十年的时间准备一次巨大的开花，然后死亡。热带亚洲的塔里波棕榈是一种令人惊讶的棕​​榈树。这棵树令人着迷和令人惊讶的是，它只开花一次，通常长达 75 年，然后在结果后死亡。Talipot，照片：PraveenP。它是最大的棕榈树之一，也是扇形的，叶子直径可达 16 英尺。它的叶子被用来写手稿，也被治疗师使用。双椰子因产生植物世界中最非凡的种子之一而闻名。它长到 25-34 m（82-112 英尺）高，叶子呈扇形。它的果实大约需要十年才能成熟，重25至45公斤，看起来像一对椰子。 双椰子，照片：WL~commonswiki，(CC BY-SA 3.0)维基百科指出，“Lodoicea（双椰子）的种子几个世纪以来一直备受珍视；它们的稀有性引起了宫廷的极大兴趣和高价，坚韧的外层种皮被用来制作碗，例如苏菲派/托钵僧乞丐施舍卡什库尔碗和其他乐器。它是地球上最高的树种。太平洋海岸红杉是最高的树木之一。该物种的高度可达 115.9 m（380.1 英尺，即比自由女神像还要高）。太平洋海岸红杉，照片：Acroterion，（CC BY-SA 4.0）不仅是最高的红杉，而且也是长寿的红杉之一，寿命可达 1,200-2,200 年或更长时间。这种树生长于美国加利福尼亚州和俄勒冈州沿海地区。地球上最高的树是海岸红杉，但最高的开花植物是澳大利亚山梣。其最高的现存标本高达 100 m（328 英尺）。澳大利亚山梣，照片：Bob Beale，（公共领域）已知最古老的标本约有 500 年的历史。有趣的是，研究表明，低强度火灾可以导致幼树生长，而不会杀死母树。古老的山梣林是世界上碳密度最高的森林之一。最古老的现存树木是一棵狐尾松，据报道已有 4,800 多年的历史，位于加利福尼亚州东部（尽管维基百科解释说）确切的位置是一个严密保守的秘密）。狐尾松，照片：James R Bouldin，（公共领域）科学家们很好奇为什么这棵树能活得这么久。它们很难种植，在花园里它们会成为根腐病的受害者。不过，它们在多岩石的土壤和少雨的地区生长得很好。老Tjikko以一种奇怪的方式古老：尽管可见的树干要年轻得多，但有机体很古老。老Tjikko，照片：Karl Brodowsky，（CC BY 3.0）虽然它是瑞典的一棵有9,568年树龄的云杉，但它不是一棵如此古老的单树，而是一棵克隆树（克隆树是一组基因相同且相互连接的树，从一棵原始树生长而来）再生出新的树干、树枝和根。Pando 看起来像许多独立的树，但它们都是一个巨大有机体的一部分，通过相同的根在地下连接起来。换句话说，下图中看似森林的地方实际上可能是一棵 Pando 树。对我来说，它是这个名单上最违反直觉的树之一。Pando，照片：J Zapell，（公共领域）维基百科指出，“作为一棵多茎树，Pando 是世界上最大的树，从重量、面积和物种来看。” Pando 估计有 47,000 根茎，这些茎看似是单棵树，但实际上是一棵树的一部分，由占地 106 英亩的根系连接起来。这些只是外行人对这些树的简单介绍；毫无疑问，专家会对这些以及许多其他自然奇观有更丰富的见解。阅读有关树木的内容并撰写这篇文章激发了我寻找资源来了解有关树木的更多信息。如果您知道什么好的，我将不胜感激您的推荐。参考资料：大英百科全书。大英百科全书，第 15 版。维基百科没有帖子

Original Summary

  1. One of my favorite pastimes is exploring old bookstores. A few months ago, I spotted a complete set of the Encyclopaedia Britannica, 15th edition, published in 1975, but that particular set was too expensive for me. A couple of weeks ago, however, I found a good deal in an another bookstore and bought it. What an experience it has been so far. There is so much in those heavy volumes that I do not know. Recently, I was reading about cosmology when, in the same volume, an entry on trees piqued my interest. I was pleasantly surprised to learn about some unusual trees and thought I should share them though it’s a bit different from what I usually write about. Let’s start with mangroves, found in coastal areas and along riverbanks. Mangroves are unusual and a bit counterintuitive: they spread seaward while also helping defend the land behind them. These trees serve as vital defenses against coastal damage.Mangroves, photo: Ariefrahman, (CC BY-SA 4.0) Their seeds grow into seedlings and when these seedlings fall, they either become fixed in the mud or float away to another site. A banyan can look like a whole forest even though it is one tree. Roots drop from its branches, take hold in the ground, and grow into new supports like trunks.Banyan, photo: Kiran Gopi, (CC BY-SA 4.0)I was surprised to learn that one such tree, with a circumference of 2,000 feet has been estimated as being capable of sheltering 20,000 people. That is a massive amount of shade. India has several giant banyans. Thimmamma Marrimanu in Andhra Pradesh currently holds the Guinness canopy record at about 5.41 acres (an area equivalent to three soccer fields); The Great Banyan in Kolkata is another famous specimen at about 4 acres. A tree covering this much area went completely against what I would expect from a tree.Banyan tree covering 4.7 acres, photo: PJeganathan, (CC BY-SA 4.0)The ombú is tree-like in size, but so swollen and massive in form that it feels almost architectural. The ombú tree attains heights of 60 feet and a spread of 100 feet and it has a wide trunk. Ombú, photo: Roberto Fiadone, (CC BY-SA 4.0)Its trunk and roots are thick enough and in some cases enough for a person to sit upon.The traveller’s tree looks like a palm, but botanically it is not a true palm. The traveller’s tree of Madagascar, fan-shaped, has a trunk up to 30 feet tall. The foliar fan consists of 30 to 45 leaves, each as much as 36 feet in length. Their fans look majestic.Traveller’s tree of Madagascar, photo: Dicklyon, (CC, BY-SA 4.0)Its leaves have hollow bases from which, reportedly, travellers could get portable water. It can hold water in leaf bases to withstand dry conditions.The talipot palm reverses the usual picture of a tree’s life: it spends decades preparing for a single, enormous flowering, and then dies. The talipot palm of tropical Asia is a surprising one. What is fascinating and surprising about this tree is that it flowers only once, often after as many as 75 years, and then dies after fruiting.Talipot, photo: PraveenP.It is one of the largest palms, also fan-shaped, with leaves up to 16 feet in diameter. Its leaves have been used to write manuscripts and have also been used by healers.The double coconut is remarkable for producing one of the most extraordinary seeds in the plant world. It grows to 25–34 m (82–112 ft) tall and has fan-shaped leaves. Its fruits take about ten years to mature, weigh 25 to 45 kilograms, and look like a pair of coconuts.Double coconut, photo: WL~commonswiki, (CC BY-SA 3.0)Wikipedia notes that, “The seeds of Lodoicea (double coconut) have been highly prized over the centuries; their rarity caused great interest and high prices in royal courts, and the tough outer seed coat has been used to make bowls such as for Sufi/Dervish beggar-alms kashkul bowls and other instruments.”The coast redwood is not just among the tallest trees; it is the tallest tree species on Earth. Pacific Coast redwoods are among the tallest trees; the species reaches up to 115.9 m (380.1 ft, that is, which makes it taller than the Statue of Liberty) in height. Pacific Coast redwoods, photo: Acroterion, (CC BY-SA 4.0)Not just the tallest but also one of the long-lived, living 1,200–2,200 years or more. This tree is found along coastal California and Oregon within the United States.The tallest tree on Earth is a coast redwood, but the tallest flowering plant is the Australian mountain ash. Its tallest living specimen stands 100 m (328 feet) tall. Australian mountain ash, photo: Bob Beale, (Public domain)The oldest known specimen is about 500 years old. Interestingly, studies have shown that low-intensity fires can lead to the growth of younger trees without killing the parent trees. Old-growth mountain ash forests are some of the most carbon-dense forests in the world. The oldest living tree that is still a single, individual tree is a bristlecone pine, reported to be more than 4,800 years old, in eastern California (though Wikipedia explains that the precise location is a closely guarded secret). Bristlecone pine, photo: James R Bouldin, (public domain)Scientists are curious why this tree lives so long. They are hard to cultivate and in gardens they fall victim to root rot. They do well in rocky soils though, areas with little rain. Old Tjikko is old in a stranger way: the organism is ancient, even though the visible trunk is much younger.Old Tjikko, photo: Karl Brodowsky, (CC BY 3.0)Although it is a 9,568-year-old spruce in Sweden but it’s not an individual tree that old but rather a clonal tree (a clonal tree is a group of trees that are genetically identical and connected, having grown from a single original tree) with regenerated new trunks, branches, and roots.Pando looks like many separate trees, but they are all part of one enormous organism joined underground by the same roots. In other words, what appears to be a forest in the picture below could actually be a single Pando tree. For me, it is one of the most counterintuitive trees on this list.Pando, photo: J Zapell, (public domain)Wikipedia notes that, “As a multi-stem tree, Pando is the world’s largest tree by measures of weight, landmass, and species.” Pando has an estimated 47,000 stems that appear to be individual trees but are part of a single tree connected by a root system that spans 106 acres.These are only a few words on these trees from a layman; an expert would no doubt have much richer insights into these and many other wonders of nature. Reading about trees and writing this post has inspired me to look for resources to learn more about trees. If you know of any good ones, I would be grateful for your recommendations.References:Encyclopaedia Britannica. Encyclopaedia Britannica, 15th edition.WikipediaNo posts

评论（默认中文）

rigonkulous 2026/04/05 06:10
我小时候深情地记得一棵不寻常的树，它位于澳大利亚西南部的卡里森林中[1]。我们在一个狂野、暴风雨的下午开车去那里，我母亲的一个朋友得到了许可和小屋钥匙，因为当时它不对公众开放——所以我们爬上围绕着树干的光滑、看似脆弱的铁柱[2]，一路爬到顶部，发现自己被困在一个火灾了望小屋里。.我们在紧实的睡袋，里面装着一罐冷的烤豆和昨天早餐的吐司，我永远记得那东西的李沙如摆动，小心翼翼地将风的共振变成一个恒定的数字8，一圈又一圈，有时以分钟为增量，逐渐变宽和减慢……但时不时地，风会发生一次大的快速扫过，树会通过一个奇怪的裂缝将其转化为更大的杠杆，如此接近的睡眠被推到了意识地平线之外，就像人们想知道的那样，从字面上看，如果这棵树最终要倒下……大约一百年后……但是，仅仅几个小时后，一切都平静了，灌木丛正在慢慢解冻，无情的太阳征服了地平线，铁横档干涸了，树木在早晨的日出中留下蒸汽，当我们迈出重力的步伐时，这个巨大的庞然大物的力量感觉越来越安全……我们很快回到地面，准备在亚林加普左右冲浪。 ..

这是一个美丽的生物，我对它节奏摆动的感知已经伴随了我几十年。 树很可爱。

[1]- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eucalyptus_diversicolor

[2]- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gloucester_Tree

An unusual tree I remember fondly as a child, is in the Karri forests of south-western Australia[1] .. we'd driven through a wild and stormy afternoon to get to it, a friend of my mother had gotten permission and the cabin key, as it was closed to the public then - and so it was that we were climbing the slippery, seemingly fragile iron posts that ringed its trunk[2] all the way to the top to find ourselves cramped into a fire lookout cabin .. we camped overnight in tight sleeping bags with a cold can of baked beans and yesterdays toast for breakfast, and I will always remember the lissajous swing of the thing, carefully turning the resonance of the wind into a constant figure 8, around and around, sometimes in minute increments gradually widening and slowing .. but every now and then, a big fast sweep would happen on the wind, and the tree would translate it through an odd crack into a bigger leverage, and that sleep that was so close gets pushed just a bit beyond the conscious horizon as one wondered, literally, if the tree was finally going to fall .. after a hundred or so years .. but still, just a few hours later, all is calm, the bush is slowly thawing out, the relentless sun conquers the horizon, the iron rungs dry out, the trees leaves steam in the morning sunrise, this great behemoths strength feeling safer and safer as we take gravitys' step .. and we are just too soon back on the ground and off for some surf out at Yallingup or so ..

A beautiful living thing which my perception of its rythmic swing has lived on with me for decades. Trees are lovely.

[1] - https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eucalyptus_diversicolor

[2] - https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gloucester_Tree

在 HN 中查看
buildsjets 2026/04/05 01:07
作为一种不寻常的树，我一直很喜欢水杉、水杉。人们只从化石记录中了解它，并被认为已经灭绝，直到 1946 年在中国的一个山谷中发现了一个小树林。它掀起了一股流行浪潮，世界各地都种植了一束，现在它们已经成熟，如果你想看的话很容易找到。 它们在凉爽至温带气候下生长良好且快速。 它们有微小的落叶针叶，不需要耙，它们会长得又高又对称，但不会蔓延得太宽。

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metasequoia_glyptostroboides

我通过邮购买了 2 颗豆芽，经过一个生长季节，它们长到了近 3 英尺高。我从 Jonsteen Nursery 邮购了他们，他们多年来一直专注于各种红木树苗。 https://sequoiatrees.com/

As an unusual tree I’ve always liked the Dawn Redwood, Metasequoia glyptostroboides. It was known only from fossil record and thought extinct until a small grove was found in a mountain valley in China in 1946. There was a wave of popularity and a bunch were planted worldwide, which are now mature and easy to find if you want to see one. They grow well and very quickly in cool to temperate climates. They have little tiny deciduous needle-leaves that don't need to be raked, and grow tall and symmetrical without spreading too wide.

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metasequoia_glyptostroboides

I started 2 sprouts I bought by mail order, after one growing season they were nearly 3 feet tall. I got them mail order from Jonsteen Nursery, they have been specializing in various redwood saplings for many years. https://sequoiatrees.com/

在 HN 中查看
andrenotgiant 2026/04/05 04:30
新泽西州普林斯顿有很多“成熟”的黎明红杉树 - 特别是在 50 年代建造的特定社区。

现在看来，该物种是在 40 年代被发现的

There are lots of "mature" dawn redwood trees in Princeton NJ - particularly in a specific neighborhood built in the 50s.

It makes sense now that the species was discovered in the 40s

cluckindan 2026/04/04 18:44
相关：不存在树这样的东西（系统发育）

https://eukaryotewritesblog.com/2021/05/02/theres-no-such-th...

Related: There’s no such thing as a tree (phylogenetically)

https://eukaryotewritesblog.com/2021/05/02/theres-no-such-th...

在 HN 中查看
orthoxerox 2026/04/04 21:27
也没有鱼这样的东西。除非你把鲸鱼、鹦鹉和 Kanye West 算作鱼。
There's no such thing as a fish either. Unless you count whales, parrots and Kanye West as fish.
在 HN 中查看
gus_massa 2026/04/05 00:04
“鱼”几乎是一个很好的类别，你只需要用核武器攻击一个不寻常的分支就到此为止。

更好的比较是“飞行者”，其中包括大多数昆虫、大多数鸟类、蝙蝠、翼龙，也许还有一些滑翔动物和幼崽动物。它进化又消失了几次。

"Fish" is almost a good category, you only need to nuke a unusual branch and call it a day.

A better comparison is "Fliyers", that include most insects, most birds, bats, pterodactyls and perhaps a few gliding and kitting animals. It evolveded and disappeared a few times.

在 HN 中查看
Loughla 2026/04/05 07:03
如果您喜欢琐事和英式幽默，那么播客“No Such Thing as a Fish”将会是您的绝佳选择。
If you like trivia and British humor the podcast No Such Thing as a Fish is amazing.

tomaskafka 2026/04/04 19:14
谢谢你！严格的等级分类系统在你实际研究细节的任何地方都失败了，这难道不令人惊讶吗？ 另请参见范畴论与原型理论。
Thank you! Isn’t it amazing how a rigid hierarchical categorization system fails everywhere you actually look into details? See also category theory vs prototype theory.
在 HN 中查看
TeMPOraL 2026/04/04 21:48
令人惊讶的是，大多数人都没有意识到这一点，即使在高等教育中，人们也相信分类法和类别，就好像它们是自然世界的财产一样。客观现实中不存在严格的或其他的类别；基本粒子没有附加元数据标签来说明它们属于什么排列以及它是什么类型。无论是在生物学中还是在代码中，分类法都是任意的——它们是由人们为了某些特定目的而创建的，并根据它们在服务于该目的时的有用性来判断。

你可能会认为，现在我们有了法学硕士，这是一个系统的实际实证证据，可以有效地驾驭现实世界的复杂性，而无需被喂养或内部开发僵化的本体论，人们最终会得到备忘录 - 但可惜。

It's amazing that most people don't realize it, and even in higher education you get people believing in taxonomies and categories as if they were a property of the natural world. There are no categories in the objective reality, rigid or otherwise; there are no metadata tags attached to elementary particles, that say what the arrangement they're part of is, and of what type it is. Whether in biology or in code, taxonomies are arbitrary - they're created by people for some specific purpose, and judged by useful they are in serving that purpose.

You'd think that now that we have LLMs, the actual in-your-face empirical evidence of a system that can effectively navigate the complexities of the real world without being fed, or internally developing, rigid ontologies, that people would finally get the memo - but alas.

在 HN 中查看
disqard 2026/04/05 00:12
事实上，在面对法学硕士的力量时，我的认识论教训之一是，任何系统中都可以出现一种“智力能力”，无论其规模/复杂程度如何。

如果您有兴趣，请查看 Rupert Sheldrake：

https://www.sheldrake.org/files/pdfs/papers/Is_the_Sun_Consc...

Indeed, one of the epistemological lessons for me when confronting the power of LLMs is that a sort of "intellectual capability" can emerge in any system, from sheer scale/complexity alone.

If you're interested, check out Rupert Sheldrake:

https://www.sheldrake.org/files/pdfs/papers/Is_the_Sun_Consc...

在 HN 中查看
j16sdiz 2026/04/05 00:35
同样的人抱怨番茄是水果，所以它一定不是蔬菜。
It's the same people complaining tomato is fruit, so it must not be a vegetable.
在 HN 中查看
robocat 2026/04/05 01:26
与此相关的是，我前几天刚刚注意到马铃薯果实（马铃薯浆果）： https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Potato_fruit

同属茄科/茄科植物的一部分还包括甜椒和茄子。帮助进一步混淆番茄植株和番茄蔬菜。

Relatedly I just noticed potato fruit (potato berries) the other day: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Potato_fruit

Part of the same Solanaceae/nightshade family also includes bell peppers and eggplants. To help confuse the Tomato plant and the Tomato vegetable further.

在 HN 中查看
adammarples 2026/04/05 01:04
嗯，不，我们在这里所说的是，如果你使用严格的、分层的分类系统（分支学），你可以说不存在像鱼这样的单系分组。也就是说，不存在包含所有事物的共同祖先的分组，并且仅包含我们通常称为鱼的事物。这个系统并没有失败，很好，它的目的是根据进化血统对事物进行分类。然而，在这个系统下，人类是爬行动物，树木和鱼类不是有用的类别。还存在其他分类系统，它们是多系或并系的，更适合常用语言，我们又得到了鱼、树、非鸟类非哺乳动物爬行动物。它们都没有错，只是用途不同、用途不同而已。这就像知道西红柿是一种水果，但没有人愿意把它放在水果沙拉中一样。当事物由于某种原因存在于多个命名系统和类别中时，人们往往会陷入困境。
Well, no, what we're saying here is that if you use a rigid, hierarchical catergorisation system (cladistics) you can say that there is no such monophyletic grouping as a fish. Ie there is no grouping with a common ancestor that encompasses all the things, and only the things, that we commonly call fish. That system hasn't failed, it's fine, its purpose is to categorise things in terms of evolutionary descent. However, under that system humans are reptiles and trees and fish aren't useful categories. There exist other systems of catergorisation, which are polyphyletic or paraphyletic, which fit better with commonly used language, and we get back fish, trees, non-avian non-mammalian reptiles. Neither of them are wrong, they're just differently used and differently useful. It's like knowing that a tomato is a fruit, but nobody wants it in a fruit salad. People tend to struggle when things exist in multiple naming systems and categories for some reason.
在 HN 中查看
cruegge 2026/04/05 01:41
如果我错了，请纠正我，但从系统发育上来说，我不认为人类是爬行动物。合弓纲（包含哺乳动物）和蜥纲纲（包含爬行动物）是羊膜动物门内的兄弟类群。
Correct me if I'm wrong, but I don't think humans are reptiles, phylogenetically. The Synapsida (containing mammals) and Sauropsida (containing reptiles) are sibling groups inside the Amniota.
在 HN 中查看
Tagbert 2026/04/05 04:47
AFAIK - 在羊膜类被应用之前，突触类最初被称为类哺乳动物爬行动物。
AFAIK - Synapsida were originally termed mammal-like reptiles before the Amniota group was applied.
在 HN 中查看
adammarples 2026/04/05 05:24
你可能是对的，我不是专家
You're probably right I'm no expert
在 HN 中查看
metronomer 2026/04/04 23:00
同意。拉图尔也有简洁的论点（评论 潘多拉的希望）
Agree. Latour's got neat arguments too (commenting on Pandora's Hope)
在 HN 中查看
mykowebhn 2026/04/04 20:40
我想说，桉树在世界各地都有种植，但原产于澳大利亚，是很不寻常的。

年轻的桉树的叶子是圆形的，并且彼此相对排列。然而，成熟时桉树的叶子呈矛状，并以交替方式排列。这对我来说很不寻常。

I would say the Eucalyptus tree, planted all over the world but native to Australia, is quite unusual.

Young Eucalyptus trees have leaves that are rounded and are arranged opposite to one another. However, when mature the leaves of a Eucalyptus are lance-like and are arranged in an alternating fashion. This to me is quite unusual.

在 HN 中查看
helterskelter 2026/04/04 22:42
有趣的是，一位邻居让我砍倒了他们的桉树，然后它从树桩上长出来了，几年后我不得不再次砍掉它。然后几年后我必须再次修剪它。现在看来我很快又得再剪一次了。此刻，这已成为一个笑话。

这些事情很艰难，但它们会成长 真的 在合适的气候下快速。

It's funny, a neighbor had me cut their eucalyptus down, then it grew back from the stump and I had to cut it again a couple years later. Then I has to cut it again a few years after that. Now it looks like I'm going to have to cut it again soon. It's become a running joke at this point.

Those things are tough, and they grow really fast in the right climate.

在 HN 中查看
helterskelter 2026/04/04 23:55
> 那我就得剪了

有*

@dang，我希望我们有更多时间来编辑我们的帖子。

> Then I has to cut

had*

@dang, I wish we had more time to edit our posts.

在 HN 中查看
bombcar 2026/04/04 21:18
我对它们的了解只是它们的枕木关系很差，而且它们会爆炸。

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OpH9gBsNEwI

All I know about them is they're bad railroad ties, and they explode.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OpH9gBsNEwI

在 HN 中查看
mykowebhn 2026/04/04 21:24
真的。尽管在它们的祖国澳大利亚，它们生长得很直。引进的树木长得不太直，导致铁路枕木状况不佳。

在它们引入的地区，它们也会通过一种叫做相感作用的方法变得相当具有侵略性，即它们将毒素引入土壤中，以防止竞争树种扎根。

当我在做的时候，桉树的木材非常非常密集，这意味着木材燃烧起来非常热。这使得以桉树为主的森林火灾变得更加严重。

（我知道我的植物学研究有一天会派上用场。我只是不知道什么时候！）

True. Although in their native Australia they grew quite straight. It's the introduced trees that grow not so straight and make bad railroad ties.

In areas where they are introduced, they also become quite invasive by practicing something called alelopathy, whereby they introduce toxins into the soil to prevent competing tree species from taking hold.

While I'm at it, Eucalyptus trees have very very dense wood which means the wood burns very hot. This makes it even worse for forest fires where Eucalyptus trees dominate.

(I knew my botany studies would come in handy someday. I just never knew when!)

在 HN 中查看
bacheaul 2026/04/05 05:39
桉树森林火灾很严重，因为叶子里充满了油，当天气炎热、干燥和有风时，它们非常易燃，火会以令人难以置信的速度穿过树顶，并跳过似乎足够宽的大型防火带来阻止它。

但同时木材也非常致密，因此可以制成很棒的篝火木材，但在森林火灾中燃烧得并不那么厉害，这有点讽刺……

Eucalypt forest fires are bad because the leaves are full of oil and when it's hot, dry and windy, they're extremely flammable and the fire races through the tops of the trees at incredible speeds, and jumps across large fire breaks that appear to be wide enough that they should stop it.

But at the same time the wood is also very dense, so makes great campfire wood, but doesn't burn so much in a forest fire, which is a bit ironic...

在 HN 中查看
awesome_dude 2026/04/05 04:19
> 当我在做这件事时，桉树的木材非常非常密集，这意味着木材燃烧起来非常热。这使得以桉树为主的森林火灾变得更加严重。

请原谅我的无知，但我了解木材的密度，这意味着树干在森林火灾中燃烧的可能性较小（桉树通过脱落大量干树皮来促进火灾）

> While I'm at it, Eucalyptus trees have very very dense wood which means the wood burns very hot. This makes it even worse for forest fires where Eucalyptus trees dominate.

Forgive my ignorance, but I had understood the density of the wood meaning that the trunks of the trees were less likely to burn in a forest fire (which eucalypts encourage by shedding large amounts of dry bark)

在 HN 中查看
ikr678 2026/04/05 12:39
矛状向下的形状使它们可以稍微旋转，以减少暴露在烈日下的表面积（并减少蒸腾/水分流失）。
The lance-like down pointing shape is so that they can rotate slightly to reduce surface area exposed to hot sun (and reduce transpiration/water loss).
在 HN 中查看
danwills 2026/04/05 09:17
我同意桉树很不寻常，我也发现它们很漂亮，尤其是那些树皮光滑的浅色桉树，比如幽灵口香糖和柠檬树，它们的树皮呈浅粉色橙色！这样的存在！）。我从未见过彩虹口香糖，但希望有一天能见到！

我住在南澳大利亚，我很惊讶地听说所有桉树都具有“叶子二态性”（这就是我寻找的，然后了解到它通常被称为“异胚性”）我当然在野外见过很多次，但它并不是所有桉树都普遍存在的。

在我看来，山龙眼、银桦和哈克树也是非常美丽的澳大利亚本土树木/灌木，但它们是不同的组：山龙眼科。还有一种令人着迷的结果小树，名为“泉东”，属于檀香科（不过，在现实生活中观察它时，它似乎仍然与桉树或金合欢树有一点关系）。

I agree eucalypts are unusual, I also find them beautiful, especially ones with smooth light bark like Ghost Gum and Citriodora, which has light pinky-orange bark! Such a presence!). I've never seen a Rainbow Gum but would love to one day!

I live in South Australia and I was surprised to hear about all Eucalypts having 'leaf dimorphism' (that is what I searched for, then learned that it's usually known as 'heteroblasty') I have of course seen it many times in-the-wild, but it is not universal to all Eucalypts.

Banksia, Grevillea and Hakea are also very beautiful Australian native trees/shrubs imo, but they are a different group: Proteaceae. And there's a fascinating fruiting small tree called 'Quandong' that's in the Sandalwood family (still seems bit related to eucalypts or maybe Wattle (Acacia) when looking at it in real life though).

在 HN 中查看
lmpdev 2026/04/05 09:30
作为在后院养着泉东长大的人，请不要在没有泉水的情况下种植泉东。 严肃的 规划

它在大约 10 年内增长了 40m，跨度大约为 200-300m^2

As someone who grew up with a Quandong in their backyard please don’t plant quandongs without serious planning

It grew 40m in ~10 years and spanned ~200-300m^2

在 HN 中查看
danwills 2026/04/05 09:45
哇！我没听说过！ 40m高！全东！？克里克！我见过最高的有10m左右。我猜他们在南澳通常不会那么大。

在你的情况下一定是理想的条件，也许它恰好是一个特别活跃/快速生长的变种！？我听说种子很难发芽（听起来它在你的财产上没有任何麻烦！）如果它占据了我家的大部分草地，我实际上会很高兴，但我认为！ :)

Wow! I have not heard about that! A 40m tal! Quandong!? Crikey! Tallest I've seen is about 10m. I guess they usually don't get that big in SA.

Must have been ideal conditions for it in your case and maybe it happened to be a particularly vigorous/fast-growing variant!? I have heard that it can be hard to get the seeds to germinate (sounds like it was working without troubles on your property!) I'd actually be kinda happy if it took over most of the grass at my place though I reckon! :)

在 HN 中查看
lmpdev 2026/04/05 09:55
是的，蓝泉东队变得相当大

新南威尔士州的特威德山谷拥有大量的水和火山土壤

编辑：原来蓝色泉东和泉东是非常不同的物种 - 我的错误

Yeah the Blue Quandongs get pretty big

Tweed Valley in NNSW so lots of water and volcanic soil

Edit: turns out the Blue Quandong and the Quandong are very different species - my mistake

在 HN 中查看
danwills 2026/04/05 10:30
哇哦，太棒了，谢谢你的澄清！今天我了解到还有另一种本地水果，名字相似，但你是对的，它是一个完全不同的物种！很高兴知道！

我想知道，既然“quandong”听起来像一个原住民词，它是否可能与南美水果中的“sapote”一词相似。我听说它的意思是“软水果”，几乎没有一个是相关物种！： 白 Sapote（冰淇淋水果，太棒了，与柑橘有关） 黑 Sapote：可能不太名副其实的巧克力布丁水果，但在我看来，完全成熟的水果仍然令人愉快，与柿子有关）我还没有尝试过 Mamey Sapote 或任何其他......值得期待的东西！ :)

好羡慕那火山土啊！阿德莱德山上的粘土很粘..我在地里种了很多年的白色Sapote，它的高度仍然不到1m:/不知道它是否会结果子..我猜应该更好地照顾土壤，这可能会让它继续下去:)

Ah wow that is awesome, thankyou for clarifying that! Today I learned there's another native fruit with a similar name, but you're right it's a totally different species! Good to know!

I wondered, since quandong sounds like an Aboriginal word, whether it might be similar to what happened with the word 'sapote' in South American fruits. I have heard that it means 'soft fruit' and hardly any of them are even related species!: White Sapote (ice-cream fruit, it's amazing, related to citrus) Black Sapote: might not quite live up to the name chocolate-pudding-fruit, but a perfectly ripe one is still delightful imo, related to persimmon) I haven't tried Mamey Sapote yet or any others.. something to look forward to! :)

I'm envious of that volcanic soil! Quite clay-y in the Adelaide Hills.. I have had a white Sapote in the ground for years and it's still less than 1m tall :/ don't know if it will ever fruit.. should care for the soil better I'm guessing, that might get it going :)

在 HN 中查看
culi 2026/04/05 02:21
哪种桉树？有 700-900 个物种，而且它们看起来毫无相似之处。

有关多样性的示例，请参阅 E. grandis、E. tetraptera、E. chataboma、E. deglupta、E. pulverulenta

有些非常高，皮肤非常光滑，有些基本上是灌木丛，有些有非常凌乱的纸质树皮；有些甚至有彩虹树皮！有些叶子很长，有些叶子很短，紧紧缠绕在圆形叶子上

Which Eucalyptus tree? There are between 700-900 species and they look nothing alike.

See E. grandis, E. tetraptera, E. chartaboma, E. deglupta, E. pulverulenta for examples of diversity

Some are incredibly tall with really smooth skin, some are basically bushes, some have really messy papery bark; some even have rainbow bark! Some have really long leaves while some have extremely short tightly wound round leaves

在 HN 中查看
jvm___ 2026/04/04 23:50
既然我们谈论的是树。只有生长在具有明显冷暖循环的地区的树木才有年轮，而热带树木则没有，判断大多数热带树木年龄的唯一方法就是自己种植
Since we're talking trees. Only trees that grow in an area with distinct warm/cold cycles have rings, tropical trees don't and the only way to tell the age of most tropical trees is to have planted it yourself
在 HN 中查看
marcosdumay 2026/04/05 01:18
生长在干湿循环地区的树木也有年轮。由于来自永久潮湿地区的大多数树木也有某种每年或半年的周期，我猜无环树在任何地方都是罕见的例外。
Trees that grow in areas with wet/dry cycles also have rings. And since most of the trees from permanently-wet areas also have some kind of annual or semi-annual cycle, I'd guess the ringless ones are a rare exception everywhere.
在 HN 中查看
addaon 2026/04/05 00:02
一棵没有年轮的树在生长过程中是否仍能合理地捕获大气中的 C13:C12 比率？或者树干内的碳迁移率是否太高，或者比率差异太小，以至于无法在靠近核心的地方进行采样并使用那里的比率作为年龄指标？
Wouldn’t a tree without rings still reasonably capture the atmospheric C13:C12 ratio as it grows? Or is the carbon motility within the trunk too high, or the ratio differences too small, to sample a bit near the core and use the ratio there as an age indicator?
在 HN 中查看
pvaldes 2026/04/05 04:02
从技术上讲，棕榈树和竹子是“非常大的杂草”。它们与草的关系比与松树的关系更密切，并且没有年轮。香蕉也只是巨型草本植物。

所以单子叶植物没有环。热带森林中的任何其他树都有年轮。它们生长在哪里并不重要。生长缓慢的物种的年轮较小，针叶树的结构也不同，但仅此而已。

Palms and Bamboo are technically "very big weeds". They are more related with grasses than with pines and never have rings. Bananas are also just giant herbs.

So Monocots don't have rings. Anything else that is a tree in a tropical forest has rings. It does not matter where they grow. The rings are smaller in slow growing species, and are different structurally in conifers, but this is all.

在 HN 中查看
imrozim 2026/04/05 00:23 deleted
[死的]
[dead]
在 HN 中查看
smusamashah 2026/04/04 18:32
旅人树看起来最有趣，像孔雀羽毛。

https://www.indefenseofplants.com/blog/2017/12/12/the-travel...

The traveller tree looked the most interesting, like a peacock's feather.

https://www.indefenseofplants.com/blog/2017/12/12/the-travel...

在 HN 中查看
pvaldes 2026/04/05 04:08
大药草。并不是真正的木本植物。
Big herb. Not really a woody plant.
在 HN 中查看
nvalis 2026/04/04 17:56
相关且也有趣： https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_individual_trees
Related and also interesting: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_individual_trees
在 HN 中查看
hermitcrab 2026/04/04 17:43
英国有不少古老的红豆杉树。有些可能已有2000多年的历史。它们通常出现在教堂场地上（也许是因为那些没有被砍下来做长弓的地方？）。

https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/blog/2025/08/ancient-yew-tr...

The UK has quite a few ancient yew trees. Some may be over 2000 years old. Often they are in church grounds (because ones that weren't got cut down to make long bows perhaps?).

https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/blog/2025/08/ancient-yew-tr...

在 HN 中查看
madaxe_again 2026/04/04 18:25
大自然的许多美好事物之一是，几乎所有事物都以某种特定的方式有趣且独特，无论是寿命、大小还是更具体的特征，涵盖所有物种、自然科学的所有领域。
One of the many nice things about nature is that almost everything is interesting and unique in some particular way, be it longevity, size, or far more specific traits, across all species, all domains of natural science.
在 HN 中查看
temp0826 2026/04/05 02:43
我最喜欢的树之一是 Couroupita guianensis，它还有其他几个名字（ayahuma、炮弹树）。成熟时，树干会长出一些美丽的花朵，可以覆盖树干（维基链接有一些不错的图片）。它原产于南美洲，是亚马逊植物萨满教中受人尊敬的树（该植物的所有部分都可以入药；在精神上，它是大树之一，自成一派）。它于 1800 年代传入印度，如今在印度享有很高的声誉和重要性。

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Couroupita_guianensis

One of my favorite trees is Couroupita guianensis, known by a few other names (ayahuma, cannonball tree). When mature the trunks grow some beautiful flowers that can cover the trunk (wiki link has a few good pics). Native to South America, it's a revered tree in Amazonian plant shamanism (all parts of the plant can be used medicinally; spiritually it is one of the big ones, an entire school of its own). It made its way to India in the 1800s where it holds a lot of renown and importance now.

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Couroupita_guianensis

在 HN 中查看
pvaldes 2026/04/05 04:16
花朵非常​​美丽，但呆在炮弹树下非常危险。
Superb flowers but very dangerous to stay under the cannonball tree.
在 HN 中查看
ks2048 2026/04/05 00:10
去年我第一次来巴西，那里的树木给我留下了深刻的印象。

来自圣保罗市中心的两个例子，

https://kenschutte.com/lima-to-rio-by-bus/images/trees.jpg

I was in Brazil for the first time last year and was very impressed with the trees.

Two examples right from downtown São Paulo,

https://kenschutte.com/lima-to-rio-by-bus/images/trees.jpg

在 HN 中查看
marcosdumay 2026/04/05 01:21
只是为了指出这一点，但这些都不是巴西本土的。它是一种流行的装饰树，但你在野外找不到它们。
Just to point, but those are not native from Brazil. It's a popular decorative tree, but you won't find them on the wild.
在 HN 中查看
pvaldes 2026/04/05 04:06
这是榕树。也是巴西雨林中常见的树木属之一。有些是本地人。其他没有。
This is a Ficus tree. Is one of the common genus of trees also in the Brazilian rainforest. Some are native. Other not.

mbeex 2026/04/04 18:18

philipov 2026/04/04 20:59
它可以帮助您学习从很远的地方识别不同类型的树木。
It's to help you learn to recognise different types of trees from quite a long way away.

nickvec 2026/04/05 06:53
对于那些好奇的人来说，世界上已知最高的活树是红木国家公园的亥伯龙树，高 381.3 英尺（116.22 米）。 https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hyperion_(tree)
For those curious, the world's tallest known living tree is Hyperion in Redwood National Park at 381.3 feet (116.22 meters). https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hyperion_(tree)

MeteorMarc 2026/04/04 23:33

bawolff 2026/04/05 00:41
有人拿到一本大英百科全书，阅读有关树木的内容，然后去维基百科查找图片并填写详细信息，这是一件令人着迷的事情。
There is something fascinating about someone getting a copy of Encyclopedia Brititanica, reading about trees, and then going to Wikipedia for pictures and to fill out details.
在 HN 中查看
firefoxd 2026/04/05 03:03
我记得当一株世纪植物刚刚在我的后院发芽时。一个月的时间，它长高了8米。在这个过程中看起来非常陌生。

[0]: https://imgur.com/gallery/what-kind-of-plant-is-this-grew-le...

I remember when a Century Plant just sprouted in my back yard. In the span of a month, it grew 8 meters. It looks very alien in the process.

[0]: https://imgur.com/gallery/what-kind-of-plant-is-this-grew-le...

pvaldes 2026/04/05 04:05
那是一朵花盛开。也不是一棵树。
That was a flower bloom. Also not a tree.

jareklupinski 2026/04/05 04:54
> 但你了解木头吗？你知道当你手里拿着某样东西时，它是木头做的，你能看出来吗？是的，那个东西。

每个人的金属书架上都应该有一本《识别木材》

> but you know wood? You know when you hold something in your hand, and it’s made of wood, and you can tell that? Yeah, that thing.

everyone should have a copy of Identifying Wood on their metal bookshelf

simquat 2026/04/04 18:36
在意大利最南部的卡拉布里亚，有一棵[0] 1000 年树龄的梧桐树。

[0]https://it.wikipedia.org/wiki/Platano_di_Vrisi

In Calabria — the very south of Italy — there this[0] 1000-years-old plane tree.

[0]https://it.wikipedia.org/wiki/Platano_di_Vrisi

在 HN 中查看
bombcar 2026/04/04 21:16
这是（曾经是？）印刷百科全书的优势——这是我从未有过的优势 真的 能够复制滚动维基百科。我认为这更多地与限制和缺乏链接有关，而不是缺乏信息（每棵树都有一篇维基百科文章）。

维基百科的潜水会议可能会越来越具体地介绍树木（受到粗花呢攻击！）；百科全书翻转会议更有可能涉及广泛的主题。

This is (was?) the advantage of a printed encyclopedia - one that I've never really been able to replicate scrolling wikipedia. I think it has more to do with the limitations and lack of linking than lack of information (each of these trees has a wikipedia article).

A wikipedia dive session is likely to get more and more specific into trees (attacked by twees!); an encyclopedia flip session is more likely to go across a wide variety of subjects.

在 HN 中查看
abound 2026/04/04 21:57
在法学硕士时代，在维基百科转储 [1] 之上构建一个 UI 来满足任何偶然翻转的特定想法并不困难。

[1] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Database_download

In the age of LLMs, it wouldn't be hard to build a UI on top of a Wikipedia dump [1] that satisfied any particular idea of serendipitous flipping.

[1] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Database_download

在 HN 中查看
hcs 2026/04/05 13:09
或者您也可以点击“随机”按钮 https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:Random
Or you can just hit the Random button https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:Random
在 HN 中查看
gryzzly 2026/04/04 22:34
不久前，我读了科林·塔奇写的《树木的秘密生活：它们如何生活以及它们为何重要》一书，我被红树林根有效地随着潮汐节奏呼吸的事实所震撼。当水退去时，压力发生变化，空气被吸入毛孔。当水进入时，压力将污浊的空气推出并密封毛孔。树很美丽。
A while back I read this book "The Secret Life of Trees: How They Live and Why They Matter" from Colin Tudge and I was blown away by the fact that Mangrove roots effectively breath with the rhythm of tide. As the water recedes, change in pressure and the air is drawn into the pores. As the water comes in, pressure pushes stale air out and seals the pores. Trees are beautiful.
在 HN 中查看
Mistletoe 2026/04/04 19:05
我喜欢想象外星人访问地球并径直从我们身边走过并与潘多交流。

> 最近的 2024 年分析证实它至少有 16,000 年的历史，有可能长达 80,000 年，使其成为最古老的生物体之一。

I like to imagine aliens visiting earth and walking straight past us and communing with Pando.

> Recent 2024 analysis confirmed it is at least 16,000 years old, with possibilities ranging up to 80,000 years, making it one of the oldest living organisms.

richard_chase 2026/04/05 00:45
我期望并想要树数据结构。

在 HN 中查看
markstock 2026/04/05 04:01
那么您需要 Samet 的《多维和度量数据结构基础》。除非您已经拥有它，否则请享受一些漂亮的（有机）树木。
Then you want Foundations of Multidimensional and Metric Data Structures by Samet. Unless you already have it, then enjoy some pretty (organic) trees.
在 HN 中查看
Guestmodinfo 2026/04/04 21:02
对于生活在热带气候的人们来说，这些树一点也不罕见。有趣的树 是的，但不寻常。世界上大多数人生活在热带气候中，因此对大多数人来说，这些并不罕见
The trees are not unusual at all for the people living in tropical climates. Fun trees Yes but unusual no. Most people of the world live in tropical climates so for most these are not unusual

estimator7292 2026/04/04 21:16

Let people enjoy things. You aren't contributing to the conversation, you're trying to shit on everyone else for finding something interesting.

t-3 2026/04/04 21:51

Pushing back against the subtle suggestion that only American and European viewpoints are normal is more an example of cleaning up shit than shitting on anybody.

lokar 2026/04/04 22:36
